goal: All conversation AND worker progress for one ADR/workplan lives in one thread (e.g. decision-adr-047, decision-wp-foo-p1-1) regardless of which operator session triggered the dispatch. Auto-followup posts go to that subject thread instead of brain-chat:<session-thread>:auto-followup.
parallelism_note: P3.1 can start as soon as P1.3 is done (does not wait for P2). P3.2 depends on both P3.1 and P2.2. Maximum parallel tasks at P3 entry: P2.3 + P3.1 can run concurrently.

phases.description: Implement a pure function that accepts a dispatch prompt string and returns a canonical subject-thread ID (e.g. 'decision-adr-047', 'decision-wp-embedded-assets-p2-3'). Rules: if the prompt references an ADR number, emit decision-adr-<N>; if it references a workplan ID + phase/task, emit decision-<wp-id>-<phase>-<task>; if it references a workplan ID only, emit decision-<wp-id>; otherwise fall back to nil (caller keeps session thread).

tasks.description: Add port interface SubjectThreadDeriver to the brain domain layer. Signature: derive_subject_thread(prompt: &str) -> Option<String>. Document matching rules in a doc-comment. File: brain-infra/src/dispatch/subject_thread.rs (new file). Done: file compiles, doc-comment covers ADR, workplan+phase+task, workplan-only, and fallback cases.
tasks.done_condition: cargo check -p brain-infra passes; doc-comment present with all 4 matching rules
tasks.status_note: False done marker — no file at brain-infra/src/dispatch/subject_thread.rs (crate does not exist). Real target: hex-nexus/src/orchestration/.
tasks.title: Design subject-thread derivation interface

tasks.description: Implement SubjectThreadDeriver using regex. Patterns (in priority order): (1) ADR-\d+ -> decision-adr-<lowercased-number>; (2) wp-<slug>:P<N>.<M> -> decision-wp-<slug>-p<N>-<M>; (3) wp-<slug>:P<N> -> decision-wp-<slug>-p<N>; (4) wp-<slug> standalone -> decision-<slug>. Return None if no pattern matches. File: brain-infra/src/dispatch/subject_thread.rs. Done: unit tests for all 4 patterns + fallback pass.
tasks.done_condition: cargo test -p brain-infra subject_thread passes (min 6 test cases)
tasks.status_note: False done marker — no derive_subject_thread fn exists. Grep -R 'derive_subject_thread' returns zero hits.
tasks.title: Implement regex-based subject-thread extraction

tasks.description: Add subject_thread: Option<String> field to the BrainDispatch domain struct (or equivalent dispatch envelope). Populate it at dispatch-creation time by calling SubjectThreadDeriver::derive on the prompt. File: wherever BrainDispatch is defined (likely brain-infra/src/dispatch/mod.rs or brain-domain). Done: all existing tests still pass; new field serialises to/from JSON.
tasks.done_condition: cargo test --workspace passes; subject_thread field present in dispatch JSON snapshots
tasks.status_note: False done marker — no subject_thread field on any dispatch struct. Grep -R 'subject_thread' on workspace returns zero hits outside this workplan.
tasks.title: Wire SubjectThreadDeriver into BrainDispatch struct

phases.title: Subject-thread derivation

phases.description: Change both types so that when subject_thread is Some, progress and result messages are posted to that thread instead of brain-chat:<session-thread>:auto-followup. Session thread receives only a brief 'see <subject-thread> for details' cross-post.

tasks.description: In BrainProgressStreamer::stream_progress (and equivalent tick/heartbeat methods), check dispatch.subject_thread. If Some(t), post progress lines to thread t. If None, keep existing session-thread behaviour. File: brain-infra/src/progress_streamer.rs (or equivalent). Done: unit test verifies that a dispatch with subject_thread=Some posts to that thread; dispatch with subject_thread=None posts to session thread.
tasks.done_condition: cargo test -p brain-infra progress_streamer passes; both routing branches covered
tasks.status_note: False done marker — hex-nexus/src/orchestration/brain_progress_streamer.rs::extract_thread_id (line 235) still strips the auto-followup suffix and posts back to the session thread. No subject-thread branch.
tasks.title: Update BrainProgressStreamer to post to subject thread

tasks.description: In BrainDispatchReconciler::reconcile (or on_dispatch_complete), post the final result to dispatch.subject_thread if Some. Also emit a one-line cross-post to the originating session thread: '✓ Worker @<role> finished — full output in <subject-thread>'. File: brain-infra/src/dispatch_reconciler.rs (or equivalent). Done: integration test confirms result lands in subject thread and cross-post appears in session thread.
tasks.done_condition: cargo test -p brain-infra dispatch_reconciler passes; result in subject thread + cross-post in session thread both asserted
tasks.title: Update BrainDispatchReconciler to post result to subject thread

tasks.description: Run cargo check --workspace && cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast. Fix any compilation errors or test failures introduced by P2.1/P2.2. Done: both commands exit 0.
tasks.done_condition: cargo check --workspace && cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast both exit 0
tasks.title: Gate: full workspace compile + test after P2 changes

phases.title: Update BrainProgressStreamer and BrainDispatchReconciler

phases.description: For dispatches that were created before this change and therefore have no subject_thread field, run a one-time migration: scan the dispatch store, apply SubjectThreadDeriver to each prompt, write the result into subject_thread if non-nil, and copy any existing auto-followup messages from brain-chat:<session>:auto-followup into the correct decision-* thread.

tasks.description: Write a CLI subcommand or standalone Rust binary (brain-infra/src/bin/backfill_subject_threads.rs) that: (1) iterates all dispatch records in the store, (2) calls SubjectThreadDeriver::derive on each prompt, (3) sets subject_thread = Some(result) where currently None and derivation succeeds, (4) persists the updated record. Dry-run flag required. Done: dry-run prints expected changes; live run mutates store; idempotent on re-run.
tasks.done_condition: binary compiles; dry-run output lists dispatches that would be updated; live run is idempotent
tasks.status_note: False done marker — no backfill binary exists. Find . -name 'backfill_subject*' returns zero hits.
tasks.title: Write backfill script: derive + stamp subject_thread on existing dispatches

tasks.description: Extend the backfill binary (or add a second pass) that: (1) for each dispatch with a now-resolved subject_thread, reads messages from brain-chat:<session>:auto-followup, (2) appends them to the subject thread with a header '--- backfilled from <session-thread> ---', (3) does NOT delete the originals (non-destructive). Done: messages appear in subject thread after run; originals intact.
tasks.done_condition: backfill run results in messages appearing in target decision-* thread; source auto-followup thread unchanged
tasks.status_note: Blocked on P1.x/P2.x/P3.1 — backfill needs a subject_thread field to backfill into and a derivation fn to call. Neither exists. Auto-followup repeatedly fires this task; that loop is the symptom, not the bug.
tasks.title: Write backfill script: copy auto-followup messages to subject threads

Done: grep -n 'subject_thread' CLAUDE.md returns at least 3 hits.

phases.title: Backfill existing dispatches

status_note: 2026-05-05: false 'done' markers cleared. No code corresponds to P1.x/P2.1/P3.x — workplan paths reference a fictional 'brain-infra' crate (real code is in hex-nexus/src/orchestration/). Commit 93368fc8 (per-decision threading) superseded this design with an operator-side stable-thread-key approach (POST /api/brain/threads/by-key/{key}) instead of dispatch-side derivation+routing. Needs re-planning against actual architecture: derive subject thread inside hex-nexus/src/orchestration/brain_dispatch_reconciler.rs and brain_progress_streamer.rs, mirroring the TS decisionThreadKey() in hex-nexus/assets/src/components/views/Brain.tsx so dashboard clicks and worker output land in the same decision-<subject> thread.
title: Route auto-followup worker dispatches to per-subject decision threads
